result:
Fleshed petition written to `/workspace/nexus-mk2/.scratch/patron-anima-eval/eval-a/reps/2/patron-baseline/engine-refresh-bug.md`.

Key calls I made on top of the thin brief:
- **Reader/decision:** the operator watching a live session, deciding whether to intervene — which makes preserved scroll/selection the core requirement, not a nice-to-have.
- **Scope cut:** fix only the engine-detail view on the Spider page; explicitly excluded other Oculus pages, cadence changes, layout redesign, and a transport rewrite (no SSE/WebSocket scope creep).
- **Solution shape:** diff-and-patch updates with stable component identity; pin-to-bottom auto-scroll only when the user is already at the bottom; no blanking between fetches.
- **Concrete acceptance:** scroll halfway up, select text, walk away two minutes — come back to unchanged scroll, intact selection, accumulated lines below.
- **Deferred to planner:** confirm the actual refresh mechanism, check for fields that *should* visibly tick, and check whether adjacent views share the broken pattern.
